data.name: AppTagline
data.value: Understand what is happening on your PC, without telemetry

data.name: VtSettingsDescription
data.value: Optional. WinSight sends only the SHA-256 hash of a noteworthy existing file to VirusTotal. No file is uploaded. MCP access never enables these requests.

data.name: VtQuotaPolicy
data.value: Community safeguards: 4 lookups per rolling minute, 500/day and 15,500/month (UTC). Personal/non-commercial use only; business use requires a VirusTotal Premium agreement.

data.name: VtApiKeyHint
data.value: The key is encrypted for your Windows account (DPAPI) and is never included in exports. WINSIGHT_VT_KEY remains available for automation and takes precedence.

data.name: VtStatusEnabled
data.value: Enabled, an encrypted key is configured for this Windows account.

data.name: VtStatusEnvironment
data.value: Enabled for this session. An environment-provided key takes precedence.

data.name: VtInvalidKey
data.value: Enter a valid VirusTotal API key (32 to 128 letters, digits, “-” or “_”).

data.name: VtSaved
data.value: Enabled, the API key has been stored securely.

data.name: VtSavedEnvironment
data.value: Encrypted key saved. WINSIGHT_VT_KEY remains active and takes precedence for this session.

data.name: VtDisabledEnvironment
data.value: Encrypted key removed. VirusTotal remains enabled by WINSIGHT_VT_KEY; remove that variable to disable it.

data.name: VtSettingsError
data.value: The setting could not be changed: {0}

data.name: ChooseCheckTitle
data.value: What would you like to check?

data.name: ChooseCheckSubtitle
data.value: Choose a check. The overview is recommended to get started.

data.name: FlaggedOnly
data.value: Show only items that need attention

data.name: FlaggedOnlyTooltip
data.value: Hides normal items to reduce noise.

data.name: ReadySummary
data.value: Ready. Select an analysis, then click Start.

data.name: RunThisAnalysis
data.value: Run “{0}” to display its own results.

data.name: NoAnalysisForTool
data.value: No result from another category is shown here. Start this check or run the overview.

data.name: DefaultGuidance
data.value: An alert is a clue, not proof of infection.

data.name: ReadOnlyFooter
data.value: WinSight never deletes or blocks automatically; firewall changes are always your explicit choice. VirusTotal is optional in Settings.

data.name: FirewallControlsHint
data.value: Block an app, or select a policy row below to allow, block, or remove it. Uncheck "Show only what needs attention" to see every policy.

data.name: FirewallEmergencyConfirm
data.value: Turn off outbound filtering and lift every WinSight block? The machine returns to audit-only.

data.name: FirewallActionAppliedNotEnforced
data.value: Saved. This block filters traffic only once firewall enforcement is enabled (an elevated action).

data.name: FirewallActionUnavailable
data.value: The firewall service is not available. Install and start it, then try again.

data.name: FirewallActionUnauthorized
data.value: You are not authorized to change firewall policy on this machine.

data.name: FirewallEnableEnforcementConfirm
data.value: Start filtering outbound traffic now? Every saved block takes effect immediately, and the apps you blocked lose their network access. Emergency disable returns the machine to audit-only at any time.

data.name: FirewallEnforcementEnabled
data.value: Enforcement is on. Saved blocks are now filtering outbound traffic.

data.name: FirewallEnforcementNotActive
data.value: The request completed, but active filtering was not observed. Check the firewall status before relying on saved blocks.

data.name: FirewallActionNotSupported
data.value: This machine has no usable outbound filtering engine, so enforcement could not filter anything. Nothing was changed.

data.name: FirewallActionRejected
data.value: The firewall service rejected the change.

data.name: BlockOutboundTooltip
data.value: Block this application's outbound network traffic via the WinSight firewall.

data.name: BlockOutboundConfirm
data.value: Block all outbound network traffic for '{0}'? It takes effect once firewall enforcement is enabled.

data.name: TrayHintMessage
data.value: WinSight is still running in the notification area.

data.name: ScanCancelledSummary
data.value: Analysis stopped safely between two steps.

data.name: InsufficientSummary
data.value: Insufficient permissions. This analysis may require running as administrator.

data.name: ProgressInsufficient
data.value: Analysis cannot run with the current permissions.

data.name: ReadingWindows
data.value: WinSight is reading Windows information. No changes are being made.

data.name: StopRequested
data.value: Stop requested, the current step will finish without being interrupted abruptly…

data.name: NoItems
data.value: No items match the current filter. This does not guarantee that the system is threat-free.

data.name: SelectFinding
data.value: Select a row to see the safe actions available.

data.name: ToolOverviewDescription
data.value: Checks the main signals: startup items, camera/microphone, connections, DNS, extensions, hosts and certificates.

data.name: ToolOverviewGuidance
data.value: Start with “Needs attention” rows. An alert is a clue: check the name, location and publisher before taking action.

data.name: ToolPersistenceDescription
data.value: Finds programs, services and settings that can return at every startup or sign-in.

data.name: ToolPersistenceGuidance
data.value: An unknown or unsigned item deserves review. Never remove a system entry without a backup and confirmation.

data.name: ToolAvDescription
data.value: Shows apps recorded by Windows as having used the webcam or microphone, including those active now.

data.name: ToolAvGuidance
data.value: If a sensor is active unexpectedly, close the app and review its permissions in Windows privacy settings.

data.name: ToolNetworkDescription
data.value: Links TCP/UDP connections to the programs that opened them and to their digital signatures.

data.name: ToolNetworkGuidance
data.value: An external connection is not necessarily malicious. First check the program, its path and its signature.

data.name: ToolDnsDescription
data.value: Shows the Windows DNS cache: domain names recently resolved by this computer.

data.name: ToolDnsGuidance
data.value: A DNS entry records a lookup, not necessarily an intentional visit. Correlate it with connections and active programs.

data.name: ToolFirewallDescription
data.value: Inventories Microsoft Defender Firewall rules without changing them. Per-application filtering is controlled separately by the opt-in outbound service.

data.name: ToolFirewallGuidance
data.value: Use the Windows tool to change a rule. Keep a recovery path before blocking a system service.

data.name: ToolOutboundFirewallDescription
data.value: The WinSight outbound-firewall service reports its requested mode, effective runtime state, and stored per-application policies.

data.name: ToolOutboundFirewallGuidance
data.value: Before trusting firewall status, verify the service registration, running state, and LocalSystem identity in Windows. Saved policies filter only after an elevated user enables enforcement; degraded enforcement never reports a block as active.

data.name: OutboundFirewallUnavailable
data.value: Firewall service unavailable. Its installation state could not be verified, and WinSight is not reporting outbound filtering as active.

data.name: OutboundFirewallAuditOnly
data.value: Service endpoint reachable; audit-only. Policies are recorded but nothing is blocked.

data.name: OutboundFirewallPending
data.value: Reached {0}, {1} connection(s). You have never allowed or blocked this app.

data.name: OutboundFirewallEnforcing
data.value: Service endpoint reachable and enforcing per-application policies.

data.name: OutboundFirewallDegraded
data.value: Service reachable, but enforcement is degraded. Saved blocks are not reported as active.

data.name: ToolProcessesDescription
data.value: Lists processes, their paths, parents, command lines and signatures.

data.name: ToolProcessesGuidance
data.value: An unsigned process may be legitimate. Open its location and confirm its origin before ending it.

data.name: ToolModulesDescription
data.value: Finds unsigned or untrusted DLLs loaded into accessible processes.

data.name: ToolModulesGuidance
data.value: This check can take time. An unknown DLL inside a signed program warrants investigation, not immediate deletion.

data.name: ToolExtensionsDescription
data.value: Inspects Chromium extensions and flags permissions that can read or change many pages.

data.name: ToolExtensionsGuidance
data.value: Disable unnecessary or unknown extensions in the browser, then verify their publisher and official listing.

data.name: ToolCertificatesDescription
data.value: Finds root certificates with risky signals: a local private key, a weak signature or a key that is too short.

data.name: ToolCertificatesGuidance
data.value: Never remove an enterprise certificate without your administrator’s advice. Compare the issuer and expected use.

data.name: ToolHostsDescription
data.value: Detects redirects that may hijack a website or block security and update services.

data.name: ToolHostsGuidance
data.value: Ad blockers also use this file. Check the domain and address before changing anything.

data.name: ToolAlertsShort
data.value: What was detected while you were away

data.name: ToolAlertsDescription
data.value: The recorded history of real-time detections: new startup items, and ransomware-like file activity. Every alert is written here the moment it fires, so one you missed is not lost.

data.name: ToolAlertsGuidance
data.value: Windows can hide notification banners (Do not disturb, or after several in a row), so this list is the reliable record — not the banners. It is stored only on this PC.

data.name: HostExternalRedirect
data.value: Redirects a hostname to an external address (possible hijack)

data.name: HostSecurityBlackhole
data.value: Blackholes a security or update domain (possible protection bypass)

data.name: CertificatePrivateKeyRisk
data.value: Trusted root holds a private key and can mint trusted certificates

data.name: CertificateWeakSignatureRisk
data.value: Weak signature algorithm ({0}) on a non-self-signed root-store certificate

data.name: GuardianDetectedNotable
data.value: A new startup item was added and is not trusted (unsigned, untrusted, or its file is missing). Review it.

data.name: GuardianDetectedSigned
data.value: A new signed startup item was added.

data.name: ToolInputDescription
data.value: Lists the kernel drivers sitting in the keyboard and mouse paths. A driver here sees every key you press, before any application does — which is exactly where a serious keylogger installs itself.

data.name: ToolInputGuidance
data.value: Only the driver Windows installs itself is expected. Touchpad and remote-desktop software legitimately appear here too, so check the publisher rather than assuming the worst — but an unsigned driver in this list deserves immediate attention.

data.name: RansomwareProtectionTooltip
data.value: Places hidden decoy files in your Documents, Desktop and Pictures, and alerts you the moment anything modifies them — that is how ransomware behaves. Nothing is written until you turn this on, and the decoys are removed when you turn it off or close WinSight.

data.name: RansomwareCanaryTouched
data.value: A protected decoy file was just modified. Nothing legitimate changes it, so this is how ransomware behaves. Disconnect this PC from the network and check what is running.

data.name: RansomwareBurstDetected
data.value: An unusual burst of file renames or deletions was detected. If you did not start a bulk operation, treat this as suspicious.
